title: Product Launch Orchestration
objective: Coordinate launch readiness from brief intake through verified go/no-go decision.
trigger: A launch owner requests checkpointed coordination for a release candidate.

stages.name: Intake planning
actions: Extract launch objectives, owner, target date, stakeholders, dependencies, and blockers.
Create the initial checkpoint log with explicit open questions and readiness assumptions.

checkpoint.name: Scope checkpoint
criteria: Owner and target date are explicit.
Launch blockers are listed or marked none.

recoveryActions: Request missing launch owner or date before continuing.

stages.name: Execution coordination
actions: Collect status from product, engineering, support, marketing, and operations readiness owners.
Update the checkpoint log with readiness status, blocker owner, due date, and escalation path.

checkpoint.name: Readiness checkpoint
criteria: Each readiness area has pass, fail, or blocked status.
Every blocker has an owner and next action.

recoveryActions: Escalate unowned blockers to launch owner and pause go/no-go.

stages.name: Verification review
actions: Verify readiness evidence against go/no-go completion criteria.
Write final decision with residual risk, owner, timestamp, and resume token.

checkpoint.name: Go/no-go checkpoint
criteria: No blocker lacks owner or next action.
Decision includes residual risk and timestamp.

recoveryActions: Stop launch and return to execution coordination.

metrics: Track completed checkpoints divided by total checkpoints.
auditTrail: Record decision owner, timestamp, resume token, and changed artifacts.

completionCriteria: All checkpoints pass or blockers are explicitly escalated.
Go/no-go decision is written with residual risks and resume token.
